content: The 27th Amendment limits the ability of Congress to raise its own pay, stating that any pay increase cannot take effect until after the next election for the House of Representatives. This means that if Congress votes to give itself a raise, the increased pay will not go into effect until after the next election, which is typically two years later. This amendment aims to prevent lawmakers from immediately benefiting from their own pay raises.
